title: Align installed runtime surface with template lifecycle commands
rawUserRequest: 开始实施 Conditional GO 评审整改计划的全部工单，严格按序完成每个工单的实现、测试、PR、合并、ai-close-work-item、分支清理和默认分支同步，全部完成后发布新版本。

declaredIntent.summary: Make the installed Adopter Runtime Surface match the repository-governed lifecycle commands before implementing Quick Install and Calibration work.

outOfScope: Quick Install release archive digest verification
Bootstrap Wizard and lifecycle schema changes
Installer decomposition beyond runtime script exposure

sources.reason: Canonical installer script copy map and managed-file validation.
Installed command surface currently lacks lifecycle entrypoints present in the repository Makefile.
Repository-level lifecycle targets define the intended command names and arguments.
Installer copy-map and generated-adopter verification patterns.
Upgrade runtime behavior and proposal contract evidence.
Adopter installation workflow and command documentation.

riskAssessment.reason: The installer exposes existing, already-tested lifecycle scripts and Make targets; no new lifecycle state transition or external production operation is introduced.

executionDecision.reason: User explicitly authorized serial implementation of the complete Conditional GO remediation plan, including installed runtime changes and lifecycle verification.

preReviewWarnings: Review that every installed command is backed by a copied script, Make target, and real adopter evidence; no future Calibration or Quick Install claim is introduced.

checkpointPolicy.reason: Record at least one checkpoint before finishing to reduce mid-task drift.

acceptance: A fresh Adopter Fixture installed from the template contains every Runtime Surface script listed by the repository Makefile.
Installed Makefile.ai exposes version, lifecycle facts, update check/propose/apply, rollback, disable/enable, and uninstall proposal targets with deterministic arguments.
Installer copy-map validation and focused regression tests fail when any required script or target is absent.
The installation and upgrade documentation describes the installed Runtime Surface and preserves the Capability Truth boundary.
Required repository and lifecycle checks pass, with no Quick Install or Calibration implementation included.
guidelines: Installed commands must be documented with their evidence boundary.
Reuse existing runtime scripts and fail-closed validation; do not create duplicate lifecycle implementations.

intent.problem: The repository Makefile exposes lifecycle/update commands, but the installed templates/make/Makefile.ai and installer SCRIPT_NAMES omit several existing runtime scripts, so an Adopter cannot actually run the documented surface.
constraints: Expose only existing, tested runtime scripts and repository Make targets.
Keep Quick Install digest, Calibration, Bootstrap, Ownership Manifest, and independent Release Evidence for their later Work Items.
Verify the installed surface in a clean temporary Adopter Fixture, not only by inspecting source files.
Do not silently claim readiness when a command is unavailable or its evidence is not run.
intent.rationale: Aligning the installed artifact with the already-governed repository runtime closes the P0 template/adopter mismatch without broadening the product boundary.

scenarioCoverage.scenario: Clean temporary Adopter receives and can discover all required Runtime Surface scripts and Make targets.
scenarioCoverage.reason: The fixture is executed as an acceptance-stage verification, while this low-risk Contract records the scope and evidence boundary before implementation.

scenarioCoverage.scenario: Missing runtime script or target fails closed in installer validation/tests.
scenarioCoverage.reason: The negative case is executed by the focused regression test before finish; it is not a preflight behavior decision.

restrictedWriteApproval.reason: User authorized the complete serial Conditional GO remediation plan, including runtime, tests, docs, PR, merge, closure, cleanup, and release.

rollbackNote: Revert this Work Item diff and restore related tests and docs.
